subject: Project Management
title: Beijing's Bird's Nest Stadium
description: A short lesson to help you apply Beijing's Bird's Nest Stadium.

hook.text: For the 2008 Olympics, China built a stadium that looks like a giant steel nest — 91,000 seats.

buildup.text: 42,000 tons of interlocking steel beams form the structure — no two beams are the same angle.

discovery.text: The original design included a retractable roof. It was cut to save money and reduce risk.

twist.text: After the Olympics, the stadium struggled for years to find regular use — a common mega-venue problem.

climax.text: The Bird's Nest became China's architectural icon — a project that declared a nation's arrival.

punchline.text: A stadium that symbolized a nation more than a sport.

quiz.question: What was removed from the Bird's Nest design to reduce risk?
